zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Wasteneys於大約1290年出生在英國爾阿爾萬利時，他的父親John de Wasteneys20歲，母親Isabella Hay18歲。他於1320年與Joanna le Brett結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1356年在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾可爾頓享年67歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Wasteneys于大约1290年出生在英国爾阿爾萬利时，他的父亲John de Wasteneys20岁，母亲Isabella Hay18岁。他于1320年与Joanna le Brett结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1356年在英国英格兰斯塔福爾可爾頓享年67岁，埋葬在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Wasteneys
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: locative name from the Norman French pronunciation of le Gâtinais (formerly Gastinois), a province occupying parts of the modern Seine-et-Marne and Loiret.

variants.name: Westney
meanings: Anglo-Saxon: Westan-ig, from westan, west, and ig, an island, meaning a Western island

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Wasteneys was born about 1290, in Alvanley, Cheshire, England, his father, John de Wasteneys, was 20 and his mother, Isabella Hay, was 18. He married Joanna le Brett in 1320. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1356, in Colton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Wasteneys est né environ 1290, à Alvanley, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, John de Wasteneys, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Isabella Hay, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Joanna le Brett en 1320. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1356, à Colton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Wasteneys um 1290 in Alvanley, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Wasteneys 20 und seine Mutter Isabella Hay 18. Er hat 1320 Joanna le Brett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1356 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Colton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Wasteneys è nato il circa 1290 nel Alvanley, Cheshire, England, il suo padre, John de Wasteneys, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Isabella Hay, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Joanna le Brett nel 1320. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1356, nel Colton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Wasteneysが約1290年にイギリスチェシュアイアアルヴァンリーで生まれた時，父John de Wasteneysは20歳，母Isabella Hayは18歳であった。1320年にJoanna le Brettと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1356年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアコルトンで67歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Wasteneys(이)가 대략 1290년 영국 체실르 앨반리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Wasteneys(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella Hay(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1320년 Joanna le Brett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1356년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 콜턴에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Wasteneys nasceu aproximadamente 1290, em Alvanley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Wasteneys, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Isabella Hay, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Joanna le Brett em 1320. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1356, em Colton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Wasteneys родился около 1290 г. в местности алванли, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Wasteneys было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabella Hay, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Joanna le Brett в 1320 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1356 г. в местности колтон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Wasteneys nació aproximadamente 1290, en Alvanley, Cheshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Wasteneys, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Isabella Hay, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Joanna le Brett en 1320. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1356, en Colton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
